issue.title: Demo is broken
issue.author: fohoizey2
issue.description:
Hi

The demo seems to be broken, nothing outputs.
discussionHighlights: Hey yeah we had to close the demo due to costs. Unfortunately will probably remain broken (unless someone were to introduce a local LLM instead)

risks.impact: Harder to verify correctness and prevent regressions

steps.title: Setup and Preparation

Start your repo walkthrough with these likely issue-related files: src/pages/_app.tsx, src/pages/index.tsx.
pitfalls: Do not assume the first candidate file is the correct edit target until you confirm the code path.

resources.title: README Context
resources.content:
## Introduction ⚡️
A weekend project to mimmick the response streaming found in https://www.perplexity.ai/

## Demo 🚀
Try it yourself at https://perplexity-style-streaming.vercel.app/


https://github.com/reworkd/perplexity-style-streaming/assets/50181239/341a267d-ccf3-48ec-b42c-89f2bb0a403f


## Getting Started with development 💻

First, run the development server:

```bash
npm run dev
# or
yarn dev
# or
pnpm dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) with your browser to see the result.
resources.description: Read this first so you understand the project purpose and contributor workflow.

steps.description: Review the issue, repository conventions, and any linked design context before writing code.

steps.title: Define the First Narrow Milestone
actions: List candidate sub-problems or contract checks the issue suggests
Choose the smallest deterministic slice that can ship first
Mark later work such as profiling, offline analysis, or learned behavior as follow-up milestones
Write down the explicit scope and non-goals for v1
Use the candidate files to define scope boundaries, for example protocol definitions in src/pages/_app.tsx, src/pages/index.tsx and implementation modules in src/pages/_app.tsx, src/pages/index.tsx.
pitfalls: Do not bundle archive, replay, profiling, and learned-pattern work into the first change unless the repo already supports it cleanly.
steps.description: Reduce the issue to one reviewable milestone instead of attempting the full roadmap at once.
validationCriteria: A narrow v1 scope is documented
Follow-up milestones are separated from the MVP
The first milestone is reviewable on its own

steps.title: Design the Core Engine Before Adapters
actions: Define a pure state machine or rule engine for the first milestone
Keep transport input, archive input, and output reporting in thin adapters
Decide what evidence each alert or finding must retain
Prefer deterministic rule-based checks for v1 over learned patterns
If the repo already has adjacent tools or schemas, inspect them first: src/pages/_app.tsx.
pitfalls: Do not let transport code or persistence concerns leak into the rule engine.
steps.description: Keep the reusable rule or state engine separate from transport, storage, and reporting.
validationCriteria: Core logic is isolated from IO concerns
Future live and offline modes can reuse the same engine
Evidence fields are explicit for every finding type

steps.title: Implement the MVP Slice
actions: Implement only the chosen v1 contract or behavior set
Reuse the project’s existing output and alerting conventions
Record enough context to explain why a rule fired
Avoid bundling offline, replay, or profiling work into the first patch unless already trivial
Treat these as likely implementation targets rather than confirmed edit points: src/pages/_app.tsx, src/pages/index.tsx, src/pages/_document.tsx, src/pages/api/chat.ts.
pitfalls: Treat the listed files as investigation targets in remote mode, not guaranteed final edit locations.

steps.description: Ship the first milestone using existing repository conventions for logs, alerts, or outputs.
validationCriteria: The first milestone is implemented end-to-end

steps.title: Add Deterministic Tests
actions: Add synthetic tests for valid flows
Add tests for missing responses, out-of-order inputs, and duplicate events
Add cleanup tests for disconnects or session termination
Use replayable or archived examples once the core path is working
Start by checking whether tests already live near: src/pages/_app.test.tsx, src/pages/index.test.tsx, src/pages/_document.test.tsx.
pitfalls: Do not invent a new test style if the repo already has a pattern for integration or replay-driven testing.

resources.title: Suggested Test Scaffold
resources.description: Generated from acceptance criteria and adapted to the detected local test style when possible.

steps.description: Test the happy path, invalid sequences, and cleanup behavior before broadening the rules.
validationCriteria: The MVP can be validated without live traffic

steps.title: Document Scope and Follow-up Milestones
actions: Document the supported contracts or behaviors
Record false-positive caveats and observability limits
List next milestones such as offline analysis, replay support, or behavior profiling
Prepare the PR description with explicit scope boundaries
pitfalls: Do not over-document speculative future work as if it is already implemented.

actions: Install and validate the baseline with `npm install` and the project’s test script if available.

projectActionGuide.title: Trace the Code Path
actions: Trace the request or event flow from handlers into service modules and shared utilities before patching logic.

projectActionGuide.title: Scope the Issue Change
actions: Translate "Demo is broken" into one small code path to change first, then expand only if the tests and issue discussion require it.
Update the nearest existing tests or create a focused regression test next to the implementation area rather than adding broad new coverage first.
Keep a short notes file or PR draft listing assumptions, repo-specific constraints, and any follow-up tasks that should not be bundled into v1.
